faqs.question: What are the most common pantry pests in Fordham homes?
faqs.answer: The most common pantry pests in Fordham include Indian meal moths, saw-toothed grain beetles, flour beetles, and rice weevils. These pests typically arrive in contaminated dry goods from grocery stores or infest open packages of grains, cereals, flour, pasta, dried fruits, and nuts in your pantry.

faqs.question: How much does pantry pest control cost in Fordham?
faqs.answer: Pantry pest control in Fordham typically costs between $350 and $550, depending on the severity of the infestation, the size of your kitchen and pantry areas, and whether follow-up treatments are needed. We provide free inspections and upfront pricing before beginning any work.

faqs.question: How quickly can you respond to a pantry pest problem in Fordham?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day or next-day service for most pantry pest emergencies in Fordham. Our technicians are familiar with the neighborhood and can quickly reach homes and apartments throughout the area, from Belmont to Fordham Manor, to address your pest problem promptly.

faqs.question: How can I prevent pantry pests from returning after treatment?
faqs.answer: After treatment, store all dry goods in airtight containers, regularly clean pantry shelves and corners, inspect groceries before storing them, rotate older products to the front, and dispose of any infested items immediately. Our technicians will provide specific prevention recommendations based on your kitchen setup and the type of infestation we treat.
